rules.statement: Context is finite; manage your context budget carefully as performance degrades with usage
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent performance degrades silently as context fills up, producing increasingly inaccurate and inconsistent outputs without warning

rules.statement: Always clear context before starting a new feature to avoid token waste
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Stale context from prior features bleeds into new work, causing confusion, wrong file edits, and wasted tokens on irrelevant information

rules.statement: Use plan mode before building a large feature to produce a comprehensive implementation plan first
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Large features built without planning result in incomplete implementations, missed edge cases, and excessive rework that burns through context

rules.statement: A skill is just a markdown file in a folder with a description — do not over-complicate it
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Over-engineered skill structures create unnecessary complexity, increase maintenance burden, and discourage skill creation and iteration

rules.statement: Use progressive disclosure for MCP tools: load only descriptions upfront, full instructions on demand
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Loading full MCP tool definitions upfront consumes 13-14% of context budget on tool schemas that may never be used in the session

rules.statement: When running out of context, save a session log, tag it, and resume in the next session
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Work progress is lost between sessions, forcing the user to re-explain context and the agent to re-discover the codebase from scratch

rules.statement: The claude.md must contain: company overview, key processes, brand voice, data structure, and tool connections
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Incomplete claude.md results in the agent lacking critical business context, producing generic outputs that do not match the company's standards or workflows

rules.statement: Commit claude.md to a shared repository so every team member gets the same context automatically
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Team members operate with divergent agent configurations, producing inconsistent outputs and duplicating effort to maintain separate instruction files

rules.statement: Skills stack: one skill can trigger another, building a library of reusable composable components
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Monolithic skills that duplicate logic across definitions become unmaintainable and consume excessive context when loaded

rules.statement: The first iteration of a skill will not be perfect; continue iterating until output is consistent and predictable
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Shipping a skill after one iteration leads to unpredictable agent behavior, inconsistent outputs, and loss of user trust in the skill system

rules.statement: Claude Code is not a coding tool; it is an AI agent operating on your file system for any digital task
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Limiting Claude Code to coding tasks leaves the majority of its automation potential untapped, missing opportunities for non-code workflows

rules.statement: Analyze Claude Code JSON conversation logs to improve agent memory and avoid repetitive instructions
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Without log analysis, the same mistakes and friction points repeat session after session, and opportunities for memory improvement are missed

rules.statement: Turn on verbose output to understand what content enters context and how the agent behaves
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Operating without verbose output leaves the user blind to context consumption patterns, making it impossible to diagnose performance issues

rules.statement: Always review every Git change after agent edits because the agent frequently gets confused
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Unreviewed agent commits introduce subtle bugs, hardcoded values, and unintended file modifications that compound over time

rules.statement: Provide the correct amount of context for the specific business; context quality determines output quality
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Too little context produces generic outputs; too much context wastes tokens and degrades performance — both reduce output quality

rules.statement: Claude Code hooks are deterministic and cannot be overridden by the agent, unlike claude.md instructions
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Relying solely on claude.md for critical guardrails allows the agent to ignore or misinterpret instructions, leading to policy violations

rules.statement: Use pre-tool hooks for guardrails, post-tool hooks for validation, and stop hooks for memory capture
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Misplaced hook logic results in guardrails that fire too late, validations that never run, and session memory that is never persisted

rules.statement: Skills are versioned, mountable instruction packages (like Docker images), not prompts or chat templates
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Treating skills as ad-hoc prompts prevents version control, reproducibility, and reliable distribution across teams and agents

rules.statement: Move stable procedures out of system prompts into versioned modular instruction bundles
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Keeping stable procedures in system prompts wastes context every session and makes updates error-prone since changes must be applied everywhere manually

rules.statement: When a procedure changes, update the skill version and every agent follows automatically
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Unversioned skill updates cause agents to run stale procedures or break existing workflows without any audit trail of what changed

rules.statement: Run /init on a codebase to create a claude.md file summarizing the project for better agent context
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Without an initialized claude.md, the agent spends significant context and time rediscovering project structure every session

rules.statement: Start small: verbose output, Git review, backups, create skills, analyze sessions weekly
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Attempting to adopt all agent capabilities at once overwhelms the user, leads to misconfigured setups, and discourages continued use

heuristics.condition: You have 50 skills but use only 5
heuristics.action: Curate relentlessly — the remaining 45 are noise degrading context
heuristics.reason: Unused skills consume context tokens on descriptions and increase the chance of the agent selecting the wrong skill, reducing overall quality

heuristics.condition: Friction keywords ('wrong', 'no', repeating mistakes) appear in conversation logs
heuristics.action: Extract the failure patterns and feed them back as explicit rules in claude.md or skills
heuristics.reason: Friction keywords signal recurring agent misunderstandings that will persist unless codified as constraints the agent must follow

heuristics.condition: MCP tool definitions consume 13-14% of context
heuristics.action: Replace MCP tool definitions with CLI-invoked skills that load on demand
heuristics.reason: MCP tool schemas are loaded into every session regardless of usage; CLI skills only enter context when explicitly invoked, saving 13-14% of budget

heuristics.condition: The agent hardcodes values in generated code
heuristics.action: Leave a comment in the diff review and have the agent fix it immediately
heuristics.reason: Hardcoded values create brittle code that breaks across environments; catching them during review prevents technical debt accumulation

heuristics.action: Compact the conversation or start a new session to restore agent performance
heuristics.reason: LLM performance degrades measurably above 50% context utilization as attention quality drops and hallucination rates increase

heuristics.condition: A feature is large (multiple tables, APIs, UI components)
heuristics.action: Use plan mode first to decompose the feature into discrete implementation steps
heuristics.reason: Large features attempted in a single pass exhaust context, produce incomplete implementations, and make debugging nearly impossible

heuristics.condition: A plan is very detailed with many discrete tasks
heuristics.action: Create GitHub issues for each task and use GitHub-triggered Claude to implement each separately
heuristics.reason: Breaking detailed plans into individual issues enables parallel execution, isolated testing, and incremental review per task

heuristics.condition: Your plugin uses OAuth in IDE but Co-work requires MCP
heuristics.action: Auto-detect the runtime environment and switch to MCP transport automatically
heuristics.reason: Plugins that hard-code one auth method fail in the other environment; auto-detection ensures seamless operation in both IDE and Co-work modes

heuristics.condition: 99% of your job has available APIs
heuristics.action: Automate 99% via API calls through Claude Code; for the remaining 1%, use a headless browser
heuristics.reason: API-driven automation is faster, more reliable, and more maintainable than browser-based approaches; headless browser is the fallback for the gap

heuristics.condition: A process can be done digitally
heuristics.action: Evaluate whether Claude Code can automate it — the question is API or browser availability, not capability
heuristics.reason: Claude Code's capability boundary is defined by interface availability, not task complexity; any digital task with an accessible interface is automatable

frameworks.name: Session Analysis Feedback Loop
frameworks.description: A systematic process for extracting insights from Claude Code conversation logs to continuously improve agent performance and reduce friction
steps: Extract messages from JSON conversation logs stored by Claude Code
Search for friction keywords: 'wrong', 'no', 'not what I wanted', repeated corrections
Categorize friction into patterns: misunderstanding, wrong file, wrong approach, missing context
Produce a weekly friction report summarizing top recurring issues
Convert each recurring issue into an explicit rule in claude.md or a skill constraint
Verify the rule reduces the target friction in the following week's logs

frameworks.name: Skill Architecture Patterns
frameworks.description: Three canonical patterns for structuring skills based on their purpose and context efficiency requirements
steps: Targeted Tool pattern: one skill performs exactly one job with clear input/output — use for atomic, frequently invoked tasks
Router Hub pattern: a meta-skill that inspects the user request for keywords and routes to the appropriate sub-skill — use for domain-specific skill collections
Progressive Disclosure pattern: load only skill descriptions into context upfront; full instructions load only when the skill is invoked — use for large skill libraries to minimize context waste

frameworks.name: Claude Code Internal Architecture
frameworks.description: The layered structure of how Claude Code assembles its context window, from system prompt to response generation
steps: System prompt: core instructions and safety guardrails loaded first
Tool definitions: MCP tool schemas and built-in tool descriptions
Skill descriptions: short summaries of available skills for routing
Conversation history: prior messages in the current session
System reminders: claude.md contents and periodic instruction reinforcement
User message: the current request being processed
Thinking: internal reasoning before generating the response
Response: the final output including tool calls and text

frameworks.name: Context-as-Life-OS
frameworks.description: A personal knowledge management approach where notes, tasks, and goals are stored as executable context that the agent can act upon directly
steps: Create a single folder as the canonical source of truth for all notes, tasks, and goals
Structure files so each is self-contained context the agent can read and act on
Tag files with metadata (status, priority, domain) for agent-driven filtering
Instruct the agent to read relevant context files before executing tasks
Let the agent update task status and create new notes as part of its workflow
Review and curate the folder weekly to prevent context bloat

frameworks.name: Handoff Prompt Protocol
frameworks.description: A structured procedure for preserving work progress when a session runs out of context, enabling seamless continuation in a new session
steps: Detect context exhaustion: agent responses degrade, compaction warnings appear, or 50% threshold is exceeded
Create a handoff document: summarize completed work, current state, remaining tasks, and key decisions made
Tag the handoff file with session ID, date, and feature name for retrieval
Save the handoff file to a known location (e.g., .claude/handoffs/)
Start a fresh session and point the agent to the handoff file as initial context
Agent reads the handoff, confirms understanding, and continues from the documented state

frameworks.name: Claude Code Business Architecture
frameworks.description: The organizational model for deploying Claude Code as a business automation layer, with clear separation of concerns between configuration, skills, connections, and execution
steps: User: the human operator who provides intent and reviews output
claude.md (brain): the central configuration file containing business context, rules, and preferences
Skills (SOPs): modular instruction packages that encode repeatable procedures
MCPs (connections): external tool integrations that give the agent access to APIs and services
Sub agents (parallel): spawned agent instances that execute independent tasks concurrently

frameworks.name: AI Operating System Architecture
frameworks.description: The full file-system layout for running Claude Code as a persistent AI operating system with memory, skills, hooks, and credentials
steps: claude.md: primary agent configuration and business context
architecture.md: system design documentation for the codebase
readme: project overview for human and agent onboarding
setup_memory.py: script that initializes or restores agent memory state
skills folder: directory of versioned, modular instruction packages
hooks: pre-tool, post-tool, and stop hooks for deterministic guardrails
credentials: secure storage for API keys and service tokens (never committed)

frameworks.name: Plugin Distribution Framework
frameworks.description: End-to-end workflow for building, testing, packaging, and distributing Claude Code plugins to users
steps: Build: develop the plugin with commands, connectors, and skills
Test: validate locally with Claude Code in the development environment
Package: bundle as a zip file under 50MB with manifest and dependencies
Upload: publish to the distribution channel or marketplace
Second testing: install from distribution and verify end-to-end in a clean environment
Connect MCP: wire up any required MCP server integrations for external tools
Iterate: collect user feedback, fix issues, and release updated versions

frameworks.name: Plugin Structure
frameworks.description: The canonical composition of a Claude Code plugin, defining the three component types and packaging constraints
steps: Commands (slash): user-facing actions invoked via slash commands that trigger defined workflows
Connectors (MCP/external): integrations with external services via MCP protocol or direct API calls
Skills (markdown): instruction packages that provide domain knowledge and procedures to the agent
Bundle as zip: package all components into a single zip archive under 50MB for distribution
Include manifest: provide metadata (name, version, description, dependencies) for discovery and compatibility

tech_patterns.name: Core Toolchain
tech_patterns.description: The recommended set of tools for an AI-augmented development workflow: Claude Code for agent execution, Obsidian for knowledge vault and dashboard, Whisper Flow for voice-to-text input, Excalidraw for architecture diagrams, and Zed editor for Git diff review

tech_patterns.name: Text Expansion for Diff Review
tech_patterns.description: Use text expansion shortcuts (e.g., via Espanso or OS-level expanders) to insert common diff review comments instantly, reducing friction in the Git review process after agent edits

tech_patterns.name: Obsidian Dataview Skill Dashboard
tech_patterns.description: Use the Obsidian Dataview plugin to create a dynamic dashboard that displays skill status, version, last-updated date, and usage frequency across the entire skill library

tech_patterns.name: CLI Skills Over MCP Servers
tech_patterns.description: Replace always-loaded MCP server tool definitions with CLI tools invoked on-demand via skills, reducing baseline context consumption by up to 13-14% per session

tech_patterns.name: Slash Commands as Buttons
tech_patterns.description: Package workflows as slash commands that function like buttons: one invocation triggers a predictable, repeatable sequence of operations without requiring manual prompting

tech_patterns.name: Phased Plan Mode Execution
tech_patterns.description: Use plan mode to generate a to-do file with numbered phases, then execute each phase sequentially with explicit testing gates between phases before proceeding to the next

tech_patterns.name: Skill Creator Meta-Skill
tech_patterns.description: A meta-skill that creates other skills: given a domain and requirements, it generates a properly structured, versioned skill file following the canonical skill template

tech_patterns.name: Apify Plus Claude Code Pipeline
tech_patterns.description: Use Apify as an API marketplace for web scraping tasks, then pipe the scraped data into Claude Code for structured processing, analysis, and action — separating data collection from data intelligence
